run_summary: MANH position is strengthening with a +16.21% five-day rally on the restructuring/Reg FD catalyst; thesis remains intact as the gap-up and above-average volume suggest the market is reading the cost discipline positively. New flag pool includes two re-entries (AVAV, BBY) that fail the re-entry bar, several legitimate exploratory candidates (CRSP, ALK), one justified conviction fade (FIVE as RATIONAL conf 4), and one structural break (PLAB). No convictions warranted in the exploratory set; CRSP and CRSP warrant small test positions to validate the sector catalyst thesis.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide gene-editing rally + same-day 8-K with governance items (likely annual meeting)
entries.thesis: CRSP is up high single digits alongside NTLA (+13%) and BEAM (+11%) — the cohort is moving together, which suggests a sector/platform catalyst (possibly clinical data, regulatory tailwind, or M&A speculation). CRSP additionally has an 8-K today with items 5.02 (officer change), 5.03 (charter amendment), and 5.07 (typically annual meeting vote results) — the 5.07 combined with 5.03 strongly suggests routine annual meeting governance, not a destabilizing event. If the group catalyst is real and CRSP has the deepest pipeline + Casgevy commercial ramp, it should outperform peers on follow-through rather than lag them.
entries.what_kills: The 5.02 turns out to be a key executive (CEO/CSO/CFO) departing under non-routine circumstances — then the move is sympathy-only and CRSP should lag peers, not lead. Also kills the thesis if NTLA/BEAM rally fades by tomorrow.
entries.reasoning: CRSP is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3 on sector-wide gene-editing rally (NTLA +13%, BEAM +11%) paired with same-day 8-K governance items (5.02 officer change, 5.03 charter amendment, 5.07 annual meeting). The flag thesis is that the 5.03 + 5.07 combination strongly signals routine annual meeting governance (not destabilizing), and if the sector catalyst is real (clinical data, regulatory tailwind, or M&A speculation), CRSP with deepest pipeline + Casgevy ramp should outperform peers on follow-through. One-day move of +9.35% is modest relative to peers, five-day move is +1.01% (very shallow), volume is 1.85x (elevated but not extreme). This is a clean exploratory position: named sector catalyst, articulate thesis that CRSP should lead not lag peers, and a clear disconfirming trigger (the 5.02 is a key executive departure, or NTLA/BEAM rally fades). Confidence 3 fits exploratory tier (6% position). The 8-K URL is provided and theses are specific (read the exhibit, identify the sector catalyst, watch Casgevy revenue). Will teach whether sector momentum playbooks work in gene-editing.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis that CRSP 'should outperform peers' rests on pipeline depth and Casgevy ramp, but the one-day move of +9.35% is indeed modest relative to NTLA (+13%) and BEAM (+11%), which weakens the claim that the sector catalyst is translating into CRSP leadership.
red_team.critique: The five-day move of +1.01% is nearly flat, meaning CRSP has gained only 1% over five days despite a +9.35% one-day spike—this suggests the move is largely noise or sympathy-only rather than conviction in a CRSP-specific outperformance thesis. The 8-K governance items (5.03, 5.07) are correctly read as routine, but the 5.02 officer change has not been verified as non-material; if the URL exhibits show a key departure (CSO, head of Casgevy program), the trade becomes a sympathy short rather than a long outperformance. The exploratory tier and 6% position size are appropriate given confidence 3, but the shallow five-day gain is a real warning that the sector momentum may not translate to CRSP outperformance.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings release filed via 8-K on 2026-06-03 (item 2.02)
entries.thesis: A sharp double-digit decline on the day of an earnings 8-K, in the context of LULU also cutting guidance and citing undisclosed headwinds, fits a justified earnings disappointment in discretionary retail. The five-day move closely matches today's move, suggesting the drop is the earnings reaction itself rather than an overshoot. Magnitude looks appropriate for a guide-down at a stock that had been priced for continued execution.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a beat with conservative guide that was misread; or a one-time charge (inventory write-down) that's non-recurring, in which case the move would be OVERDONE rather than RATIONAL.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The bull thesis assumes the 8-K exhibits will confirm a guide-down, but the flag's own 'what_kills' scenario—a one-time charge or beat with conservative guidance—is plausible and would reverse the entire rationale. Bear case: The haiku reasoning treats the -13.78% move as 'justified' by an assumed guide-cut, but the flag itself acknowledges that the exhibits have not yet been reviewed. The five-day move (-14.55%) matching the one-day move (move_pct -13.78%) proves only that the stock moved sharply on one day; it does not prove that move was correct. The thesis leans heavily on sympathy with LULU and an assumption that FIVE's own earnings will mirror LULU's headwinds, but FIVE and LULU serve different customer bases and operate different supply chains—sympathy is not causation. The conviction sizing at confidence 4 is premature when the actual 8-K contents are not yet verified. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: FIVE is flagged RATIONAL conf 4 (very high confidence): a -13.78% one-day drop on same-day earnings 8-K (item 2.02) paired with the LULU guide-cut shock yesterday creates a compounding discretionary retail downturn. The five-day move (-14.55%) nearly matches the one-day move, indicating this is the earnings reaction itself, not an overshoot. Magnitude is justified by a likely guide-down in the context of demonstrated consumer softness (LULU's disclosure of undisclosed headwinds). The analyst thesis explicitly flags that sympathy-plus-own-print is a low-fade scenario and that management commentary on tariff/consumer headwinds will likely mirror LULU's language. Confidence 4 warrants conviction sizing; the setup is sharp and the catalyst is concrete (8-K exhibits will show comp-store miss and/or guide cut). This is exactly the kind of high-conviction read the conviction tier was designed for.)

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis assumes the 8-K exhibits will confirm a guide-down, but the flag's own 'what_kills' scenario—a one-time charge or beat with conservative guidance—is plausible and would reverse the entire rationale.
red_team.critique: The haiku reasoning treats the -13.78% move as 'justified' by an assumed guide-cut, but the flag itself acknowledges that the exhibits have not yet been reviewed. The five-day move (-14.55%) matching the one-day move (move_pct -13.78%) proves only that the stock moved sharply on one day; it does not prove that move was correct. The thesis leans heavily on sympathy with LULU and an assumption that FIVE's own earnings will mirror LULU's headwinds, but FIVE and LULU serve different customer bases and operate different supply chains—sympathy is not causation. The conviction sizing at confidence 4 is premature when the actual 8-K contents are not yet verified.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-03 with officer departure (5.02) and Reg FD disclosure (7.01)
entries.thesis: ALK has now dropped sharply over five days and added another leg lower on a same-day 8-K disclosing officer departure plus Reg FD. CFO/operational departures mid-quarter at airlines tend to precede guidance issues; combined with the prior week's slide, the market may still be underpricing follow-through if the departure is abrupt rather than planned retirement. Small-cap airline at ~$4.7B with no buffer.
entries.what_kills: Departure is a planned CEO/board retirement with successor in place, or Reg FD item is a routine investor conference deck with no negative content — then today's drop is the capitulation low
entries.reasoning: ALK is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3 on officer departure 8-K (5.02 + 7.01 Reg FD) paired with extended five-day decline (-8.92%) in a small-cap airline. The flag thesis is sharp—CFO/operational departures mid-quarter at airlines often precede guidance issues, and small-cap airlines have no buffer. However, confidence is only 3, and the setup requires reading the 8-K exhibits to distinguish a material departure (without successor named) from a planned retirement. The five-day decline is already substantial (-8.92%), and entry here is betting on follow-through from what may already be priced in. Without seeing the actual 8-K text (no exhibit URL provided in the flag), this is below the exploratory bar—too much uncertainty about whether the 5.02 is 'abrupt rather than planned.' Watch for the 8-K detail and re-flag if the Reg FD item shows soft guidance commentary or successor vacancy.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings report 5/27 with separate 8.01 disclosure, compounded by XLY weakness today
entries.thesis: DKS reported on 5/27 (Items 2.02 + 8.01 + 9.01) and the stock is now down meaningfully cumulatively. XLY is -2.22% today, so today's leg is partly sector sympathy on top of an already-soft earnings reaction. The 8.01 catch-all alongside 2.02 sometimes signals a separately disclosed item (Foot Locker integration update is the obvious candidate given recent M&A). Magnitude looks consistent with weak consumer tape + integration overhang — not obviously overdone or underdone.
entries.what_kills: Sharp reversal if the 8.01 disclosure turns out to be benign (e.g., a buyback authorization rather than guidance cut)
entries.reasoning: DKS is flagged RATIONAL conf 3, post-earnings drift lower on weak consumer tape (XLY -2.22% today). The thesis is thin: stock reported 5/27, 8.01 disclosure likely signals Foot Locker integration update, magnitude is consistent with sector weakness. No directional call is made—'not obviously overdone or underdone' is explicitly neutral. Confidence 3 + no clear directional edge + XLY sector weakness as the primary driver (systemic, not idiosyncratic to DKS) does not justify entry. If the 8.01 turns out to be benign (buyback), the stock could reverse, but that's guesswork. This is a 'wait for more signal' setup. Skip.

entries.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
entries.thesis: BBY printed earnings (8-K 2.02 on 5/28) and has now extended sharply over the trailing week while XLY is red on the day. A retailer with structural CE-demand headwinds and tariff exposure rallying this hard into a soft consumer tape suggests squeeze/positioning dynamics layered on a genuine beat — the magnitude of the multi-day move likely overshoots the fundamental delta. Fade candidates here typically retrace 20-30% of the post-print run.
entries.what_kills: The 8-K transcript reveals a major comp-sales acceleration or AI-PC refresh cycle commentary that justifies a structural rerate; continued sector-wide retail strength
entries.reasoning: BBY is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 and also appears as a re-entry warning (closed 0 days ago for a gain, confidence 3, horizon exit). The new flag repeats the OVERDONE thesis: post-earnings continuation extending sharply over trailing week (+27.47% five-day move) while XLY is red, suggesting positioning/squeeze layered on a beat, with typical fade scenarios expecting 20-30% retrace. However, re-entry discipline applies: confidence is only 3, so re-entry is automatic downgrade to WATCH per execution layer rule. Additionally, the new flag cites no information that did NOT exist at the prior exit—the same multi-day run, same sector backdrop, same magnitude-overshoot logic. The horizon for the original position has expired (5d grading window), and this re-flag is the same thesis. Re-entry confidence must be ≥4 with genuinely new information to override the re-entry bar. Neither condition is met. WATCH and wait for a fade confirmation (reversal on elevated volume, sell-side downgrades, or stall without new upside news) before re-entry.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
entries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
entries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
entries.reasoning: PLAB is flagged RATIONAL conf 4: -36.42% single-day collapse on 8x volume following earnings 8-K, with -31.73% already baked in over five days. The flag thesis is that this is a genuine miss + guide cut / customer loss / margin collapse, not an overreaction, because pre-print informed selling suggests informed money knew something. The confidence is high (4) and the signal is real (structural reset in a small semi-equipment supplier). However, SKIP is correct here because the thesis is diagnostic, not prescriptive: the flag explicitly says 'hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers,' and the outcome depends entirely on whether the move confirms a structural break (then HOLD at current price) or is a panicked overreaction (then SNAP back). The flag is saying the magnitude is RATIONAL relative to the information, not that a trade should be entered at the current collapsed price. Buying after a 36% gap-down with no clarity on the actual miss magnitude is high-risk small-cap idiosyncratic. Better to wait for analyst downgrades/commentary clarifying whether the miss is truly structural (in which case current price may be fair) or transient. Skip for now.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
entries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
entries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
entries.reasoning: AVAV is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 (Iran missile headline + defense sector bid, 30.83% five-day move, 3.28x volume, no company-specific 8-K or contract award). This name also triggers a re-entry warning: closed 2 days ago for a -5.31% loss, prior flag was OVERDONE conf 3, exit reasoning was 'thesis broken because the move has overextended past the point where geopolitical upside can justify hold; historical precedent is 30-50% retrace within two weeks absent contract award.' Re-entry discipline applies: confidence must be ≥4 and information must be genuinely new. The new flag repeats the identical OVERDONE thesis at the same confidence level (3), citing the same catalyst (Iran missile headlines, defense sector bid, no 8-K, no contract). No new information has emerged; the move has only extended further (+30.83% five days, up from the 10-15% at entry). The re-entry bar cannot be cleared because: (1) confidence is 3, not ≥4; (2) the thesis is identical to the one that justified the exit, and the move has only gotten worse for a re-entry. This is exactly the kind of chronic re-flagging of a weak thesis that re-entry discipline exists to prevent. WATCH and avoid re-entry.

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.thesis: NTLA leads the gene-editing group up double digits on elevated but not extreme volume, with empty catalyst_signals. Without an 8-K, a press release in news flow, or a conference data point I can point to, I cannot determine whether this is a clinical data leak, M&A speculation, or short-covering in a beaten-down name. Both a bull case (real catalyst, follow-through) and a bear case (squeeze fades) are plausible but neither articulates at conf 3+ without more evidence.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses tomorrow with no follow-through and no identified catalyst — confirms squeeze/noise.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.thesis: ITA is up nearly 3% and Kratos, AVAV, MRCY are all up mid-to-high single digits. The counterintuitive part: Trump-reluctant-to-resume-Iran-war should pressure defense, yet the group is bid. More likely explanation is a budget/appropriations catalyst, a specific drone/unmanned program award, or rotation into industrials (XLI +1.2%). With volume actually below average on KTOS and AVAV, this looks like a coordinated rotation/multi-day positioning rather than a news spike. Magnitude appears appropriate to a sector rotation move.
entries.what_kills: Stocks reverse with no identifiable catalyst — would suggest a one-day squeeze rather than rotation.

entries.name: GameStop Corporation
entries.thesis: GME up ~6% on 1.88x volume the day after an earnings 8-K. Cannot tell from filing codes alone whether print was actually good or whether this is retail/meme positioning. GME's price action has been decoupled from fundamentals for years; without reading the actual release, a directional call past 'reacted positively' would be guessing.

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Healthcare/biotech sector strength; 5/29 8-K appears to be annual meeting (5.07) and director election (5.02), not today's driver
entries.thesis: EXEL +6.3% on below-average volume in a strong biotech tape. The 5.02 + 5.07 8-K is dated 5/29 (annual meeting director slate — routine), not today's catalyst. Move likely reflects XBI strength plus a possible clinical/pipeline data point not visible here. Magnitude is in line with quality biotech moving with a hot XBI day.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with SMH/AVGO semi rally; no 8-K filed
entries.thesis: ONTO ripping alongside SMH +4% and AVGO +4.7% with no company-specific 8-K. Move magnitude is roughly 2x the sector ETF, which is consistent with higher-beta semi-cap equipment names that typically amplify SMH moves. Nothing on the tape suggests this is overdone or underdone — it's a beta trade.
entries.what_kills: An ONTO-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade with raised PT, contract win, M&A chatter) emerging post-close — would reclassify as UNDERDONE if magnitude exceeded news

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS up sharply but five-day still negative, suggesting today is a recovery within a downtrend driven by the broader semi-cap rally (SMH +4%). Magnitude reasonable given the bounce-back math and beta. No catalyst signals.
entries.what_kills: AEIS-specific announcement (guidance reset, customer concentration disclosure) emerging — would force reclassification

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI biotech selloff; no 8-K
entries.thesis: PTCT down hard but five-day is positive and volume is BELOW average (0.75x). XBI -4.4% explains roughly half the move, leaving a residual that could be idiosyncratic or simply beta-amplified. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no headline visible. Cannot articulate either a bull (oversold bounce) or bear (specific catalyst) case at conf 3+ without knowing what dragged XBI.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.thesis: BEAM -7% on a day XBI is -4.4% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap pre-revenue gene editing name. Volume is below average (0.87x), arguing against panic selling. Magnitude appears to match sector pressure rather than name-specific bad news.
entries.what_kills: An overnight 8-K on a pipeline setback or partnership termination would reclassify as UNDERDONE

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly met coal price action or peer sympathy
entries.thesis: Met coal name extending a strong multi-day rally on no company-specific catalyst visible to us. Could be coking coal price strength, China stimulus chatter, or short squeeze dynamics — none of which I can verify from the data provided. Volume only roughly average, which argues against a fundamental catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Peers flat or down while AMR runs alone, suggesting idiosyncratic technical action

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma
entries.thesis: BBIO filed an 8.01 + 9.01 on 5/27 (likely a clinical or commercial update on Attruby/acoramidis) and has drifted down on >2.5x volume into a soft XBI tape (-2.25%). The volume signature suggests material news but the modest price reaction is ambiguous — could be a digestion phase before a larger move either direction. Without reading the 8.01 exhibit, I can't tell if it's a script update (likely market-moving) or routine.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is administrative (e.g., conference presentation slides), making this just XBI sympathy

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible HVAC/housing demand concern or peer-driven rotation
entries.thesis: WSO down ~4% in a tape where SPY is +0.55% and with no 8-K — but XLU -1.1% and rate-sensitive/housing-exposed names appear to be the losers today. Could be tied to housing/HVAC demand concerns or a sell-side note we can't see. Without a visible catalyst, can't articulate either bull (oversold/buy-the-dip) or bear (fundamental break) at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Confirms it's just sector rotation and snaps back tomorrow
